title: VCSH ETF Review: Vanguard Short-Term Corp Bond
description: Complete review of Vanguard Short-Term Corporate Bond ETF (VCSH). Covers expense ratio, yield, duration, vs IGSB & SPSB. Best for fixed-income allocations.

mainEntity.name: What is the expense ratio for VCSH?

acceptedAnswer.text: VCSH has an ultra-low expense ratio of 0.03%, making it one of the most cost-effective short-term corporate bond ETFs available.

mainEntity.name: What is the yield of VCSH?

acceptedAnswer.text: As of our latest data, VCSH has a dividend yield of approximately 4.34%. Yields fluctuate based on interest rates and bond prices.

mainEntity.name: What is the duration of VCSH?

acceptedAnswer.text: VCSH has an average duration of around 2.7 years. This means the fund's price is relatively insulated from interest rate volatility compared to long-term bonds.

mainEntity.name: How does VCSH compare to IGSB and SPSB?

acceptedAnswer.text: VCSH, IGSB, and SPSB all target short-term investment-grade corporate bonds. VCSH generally has comparable yields and slightly lower or matching fees (VCSH: 0.03%, IGSB: 0.04%, SPSB: 0.04%).

mainEntity.name: Is VCSH a good investment for conservative portfolios?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, VCSH is often considered suitable for conservative allocations because it holds high-quality, investment-grade corporate bonds with short maturities, providing better yield than cash while minimizing interest rate and credit risk.
